description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a camouflage paint scheme featuring green, brown, and gray tones, and prominent black-and-white cross insignias on the wings and tail. The aircraft has two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically, connected by struts, and a single wooden propeller mounted at the nose. The fuselage is streamlined and tapers toward the tail, which features a conventional tail assembly with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is open and positioned just behind the upper wing, with visible engine components and exhaust pipes exposed at the front. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft, likely from the World War I era.
description: A vintage biplane with camouflage paint, open cockpit, and black-and-white cross insignias, featuring stacked wings and fixed landing gear.
description_view_0: The left-front perspective highlights the exposed engine, wooden propeller, and the cross insignias on the upper and lower wings.
description_view_1: The rear view shows the twin horizontal stabilizers, single vertical stabilizer, and the full span of the biplane's wings.
description_view_2: The right-front angle displays the cockpit opening, engine details, and the cross insignias on the wings and tail.
description_view_3: The direct front view emphasizes the propeller, engine, and the symmetrical arrangement of the biplane's wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit positioned just behind the upper wing, forward on the fuselage.
